The distance an object is from the ground after being tossed from a hot air balloon 770 feet in the air is a function of time: f(t) where f(t) is height and t is the amount of time the object has been in the air. Predict the height of the object after 5.5 seconds.

Predict the height of the object after 5.5 seconds.

Solve the problem.

A marine biologist determines that the size, p, of a population of crabs, after t days can be modeled by the function   Assuming that this model continues to be accurate, when will this population become extinct? (Round to the nearest day.)

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

The wind speed is tracked as a storm approaches. The maximum speed w of wind gusts, in miles per hour, t hours after midnight on the night of an approaching storm is given in the figure.

a. Find the slope of the secant line that passes through points (1, 17.2) and (3, 26.9). Interpret your answer as an average rate of change over the interval 1 ≤ t ≤ 3.
b. Repeat the procedure outlined in part (a) for the secant line that passes through points (7, 34.4) and 
c. Notice that the curve in the figure is generally increasing until t = 6, and is generally decreasing after  Give a plausible explanation for this behavior.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. The wind speed is tracked as a storm approaches. The maximum speed w of wind gusts, in miles per hour, t hours after midnight on the night of an approaching storm is given in the figure. a. Find the slope of the secant line that passes through points (1, 17.2) and (3, 26.9). Interpret your answer as an average rate of change over the interval 1 ≤ t ≤ 3. b. Repeat the procedure outlined in part (a) for the secant line that passes through points (7, 34.4) and (8, 31). c. Notice that the curve in the figure is generally increasing until t = 6, and is generally decreasing after t = 6. Give a plausible explanation for this behavior.

choices.label: A

a. 9.7, the wind is increasing in speed at an average rate of 9.7 mph per hour
b. -3.4, the wind speed is decreasing in speed at an average rate of 3.4 mph per hour 
c. The storm reached its closest point about 6:00 a.m. and is moving away after that.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

In the formula N = Iekt, N is the number of items in terms of an initial population I at a given time t and k is a growth constant equal to the percent of growth per unit time. How long will it take for the population of a certain country to double if its annual growth rate is 6.1%? Round to the nearest year.

In the formula N = Ie kt , N is the number of items in terms of an initial population I at a given time t and k is a growth constant equal to the percent of growth per unit time. How long will it take for the population of a certain country to double if its annual growth rate is 6.1%? Round to the nearest year.

Solve the problem.

An investment of P dollars is deposited in a savings account that is compounded quarterly with an annual interest rate of r, where r is expressed as a decimal. The amount of money A in the account after t years is given by A = P (1 + r/4)4t. Use this equation to determine the time it takes for an investment of $10,000 to increase to $12,000 if it is placed in an account that is compounded quarterly with an annual interest rate of 1.6%. Round to the nearest tenth.

questions.solution_html:
A hyperbola with centre at the origin and vertices at (±2, 0). 
Its asymptotes are y = ±  x.
questions.solution_text: A hyperbola with centre at the origin and vertices at (±2, 0). Its asymptotes are y = ± (3/2) x . Short Description: A graph of a hyperbola whose center lies at origin and vertices lies at (negative 2, 0) and (2, 0). Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 8 to 8 in increments of 1 and the y axis ranges from negative 8 to 8 in increments of 1. The branch on the passes through points (negative 2, 0), (negative 4, 5.2), and (negative 4, negative 5.2). The branch on the right passes through points (2, 0), (4, 5.2), and (4, negative 5.2). Two dashed lines for y = negative three halves x and y = three halves x are the asymptotes of the hyperbola. Both the lines pass through the origin. The values used in the description are approximate.

questions.prompt_html: Which of the graphs below are functions of x?
questions.prompt_text: Which of the graphs below are functions of x? Short Description: A graph (a) shows an upward opening parabola with vertex in the third quadrant. The parabola slopes upward to the second quadrant on the left and slopes upward in the first through the fourth quadrant on the right. Short Description: A graph (b)shows a curve that is concave down left to the origin and concave up right to the origin. Long Description: The curve slopes upward from the third quadrant, goes to maximum value in the second quadrant, and then slopes downward to the fourth quadrant passing through the origin. The curve goes to a minimum value in the fourth quadrant and then turns upward to move to the first quadrant. Short Description: A graph (c) shows a rightward opening parabola with vertex in second quadrant. The curve moves to the first quadrant at the top and moves to the fourth quadrant through the third quadrant at the bottom. Short Description: A graph (d) shows a concave up rectilinear graph consisting of three straight lines. Long Description: A line parallel to x axis lies in the third and fourth quadrants and meets other two straight lines at its end points. At one end point, the line parallel to x axis meets a line of positive slope that lies in first and fourth quadrants. At another end point, the line parallel to x axis meets another line of negative slope that lies in second and third quadrants.

choices.label: E
choices.html: None of the above graphs are functions of x.
choices.text: None of the above graphs are functions of x .

questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = - sin(πx). What is the period of this function?
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = - sin(πx). What is the period of this function?
questions.solution_html: The period of f is 2.
questions.solution_text: The period of f is 2. Short Description: A graph shows a sinusoidal periodic function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5 to 5 in increments of 0.5, and the y axis ranges from negative 1 to 1 in increments of 0.5. The curve slopes upward from (negative 5, 0) to (negative 4.5, 1), slopes downward to (negative 4, 0), slopes down further to (negative 3.5, negative 1), and slopes upward to (negative 3, 0). The cycle repeats.

feedback_html.solution: The period of f is 2.

Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5 to 5 in increments of 0.5, and the y axis ranges from negative 1 to 1 in increments of 0.5. The curve slopes upward from (negative 5, 0) to (negative 4.5, 1), slopes downward to (negative 4, 0), slopes down further to (negative 3.5, negative 1), and slopes upward to (negative 3, 0). The cycle repeats.

questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = tan (x/2). What is the period of this function?
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = tan (x/2). What is the period of this function?
questions.solution_html: period = 2π
questions.solution_text: period = 2π Short Description: A graph of a periodic function shows a tangent function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5.5 pi to 5.5 pi in increments of pi over 2, and the y axis ranges from negative 9 to 9 in increments of 1. The period of the function is 2 pi, so the curve repeats itself after each 2 pi interval on the x axis. The curve starts from the left at negative 5.5 pi and rises through the point (negative 5.5 pi, 1) and (negative 5 pi, infinity), then a new tangent curve again starts from (negative 5 pi, negative infinity), and rises through points (negative 4.5 pi, 1), (negative 3.5 pi, 1), and (negative 3 pi, infinity). The curve repeats itself in the same manner on the entire x axis to the right of (5.5 pi, negative 1). The asymptotes to the curves are parallel to the y axis and they pass through points (negative 3 pi, 0), (negative pi, 0), (pi, 0), and (3 pi, 0).

feedback_html.solution: period = 2π

images.alt: Short Description: A graph of a periodic function shows a tangent function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5.5 pi to 5.5 pi in increments of pi over 2, and the y axis ranges from negative 9 to 9 in increments of 1. The period of the function is 2 pi, so the curve repeats itself after each 2 pi interval on the x axis. The curve starts from the left at negative 5.5 pi and rises through the point (negative 5.5 pi, 1) and (negative 5 pi, infinity), then a new tangent curve again starts from (negative 5 pi, negative infinity), and rises through points (negative 4.5 pi, 1), (negative 3.5 pi, 1), and (negative 3 pi, infinity). The curve repeats itself in the same manner on the entire x axis to the right of (5.5 pi, negative 1). The asymptotes to the curves are parallel to the y axis and they pass through points (negative 3 pi, 0), (negative pi, 0), (pi, 0), and (3 pi, 0).

questions.solution_html: period = , amplitude = 2
questions.solution_text: period = (2π/3) , amplitude = 2 Short Description: A graph of a periodic cosine function as a sinusoidal curve. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 2.75 pi to 2.5 pi in increments of pi over 4, and the y axis ranges from negative 3.75 to 3.75 in increments of 0.75. The amplitude of the curve is 2, so the entire curve lies between negative 2 and 2 on the y axis with a period of 2 pi over 3. The curve starts from the left of (negative 2.75 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 2.39 pi, 2) then falls through points (negative 2.22 pi, 0), reaches its minimum at (negative 2.06 pi, 2) and then rises through the point (negative 1.89 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 1.72 pi, 2). The curve repeats itself in the same fashion with a period of 2 pi over 3 and continues to the right of 2 pi on the positive x axis.

questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = |sin x|, - 2π ≤ x ≤ 2π.
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = |sin x|, - 2π ≤ x ≤ 2π.
questions.solution_text: Short Description: A graph of a periodic function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 2.5 pi to 2.5 pi in increments of pi over 4. The y axis ranges from negative 0.75 to 1.75 in increments of 0.25. The amplitude of the curve is 1 and entire curve lies above the x axis. The curve starts from the point (negative 2 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 1.5 pi, 1) then falls to the point (negative pi, 0), and again reaches its maximum at (negative 0.5 pi, 1). The period of the curve is pi, so the curve repeats itself after each pi interval on the x axis and terminates at point (2 pi, 0).

images.alt: Short Description: A graph of a periodic function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 2.5 pi to 2.5 pi in increments of pi over 4. The y axis ranges from negative 0.75 to 1.75 in increments of 0.25. The amplitude of the curve is 1 and entire curve lies above the x axis. The curve starts from the point (negative 2 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 1.5 pi, 1) then falls to the point (negative pi, 0), and again reaches its maximum at (negative 0.5 pi, 1). The period of the curve is pi, so the curve repeats itself after each pi interval on the x axis and terminates at point (2 pi, 0).

questions.prompt_html:
Write Converse and Contrapositive
Write the converse and contrapositive.
questions.prompt_text: 1) If it rains today, then it will snow tomorrow. A) Converse: If it snows tomorrow, then it will rain today. Contrapositive: If it does not snow tomorrow, then it will not rain today. B) Converse: If it snows tomorrow, then it will rain today. Contrapositive: If it does not rain today, then it will not snow tomorrow. C) Converse: If it does not snow tomorrow, then it will not rain today. Contrapositive: If it snows tomorrow, then it will rain today. D) Converse: If it snows tomorrow, then it will not rain today. Contrapositive: If it does not snow tomorrow, then it will rain today.

questions.prompt_html:
Write Converse and Contrapositive
Write the converse and contrapositive.
questions.prompt_text: 2) If angle ABC is obtuse, then its measure is greater than 90° and less than 180° . A) Converse: If the measure of angle ABC is greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is obtuse. Contrapositive: If the measure of angle ABC is not greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is not obtuse. B) Converse: If the measure of angle ABC is not greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is not obtuse. Contrapositive: If the measure of angle ABC is greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is obtuse. C) Converse: If the measure of angle ABC is greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is obtuse. Contrapositive: If angle ABC is not obtuse, then its measure is not greater than 90° and less than 180° . D) Converse: If angle ABC is not obtuse, then its measure is not greater than 90° and less than 180° . Contrapositive: If the measure of angle ABC is not greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is not obtuse.

questions.prompt_text: 4) A power plant is located on a river that is 700 feet wide. Write a function C(x) that gives the cost of laying the cable in terms of the distance x. 1 mi 700 ft A) C(x) = 175 x2 + 7002 + 150(5280 - x) B) C(x) = 175 x2 + 7002 + 150(1 - x) C) C( ) 150 2 7002 175(5280 ) D) C( ) 175(700 ) 150(1 )

questions.prompt_text: 5) From a 38-inch by 38-inch piece of metal, squares are cut out of the four corners so that the sides can then be folded up to make a box. A) V = 4x3 - 152x2 + 1444x: 0 < x < 19 B) V = 2x3 - 114x2 + 1444x: x >0 C) V = 4x3 - 152x2 + 1444x: x < 38 D) V = 2x3 - 114x2 + 38x: 0 < x < 19

questions.prompt_text: 1) The function f(t) = -0.13t2 + 0.52t + 30.2 models the U.S. population in millions, ages 65 and older, where t represents years after 1990. The function g(t) = 0.5t2 + 11.85t + 107.6 models the total yearly cost of Medicare in billions of dollars, where t represents years after 1990. What does the function g f represent? Find g f (10). A) Cost per person in thousands of dollars. $12.33 thousand B) Cost per person in thousands of dollars. $0.18 thousand C) Cost per person in thousands of dollars. $0.08 thousand D) Cost per person in thousands of dollars. $9.26 thousand

questions.prompt_text: 2) After being in business for t years, a manufacturer of radios is making 1500 + 400t + 30t2 units per year. The sales price in dollars per unit is calculated by using the formula 30 + 3t. Write a formula for the manufacturerʹs yearly revenue R(t) after t years. A) R(t) = (1500 + 400t + 30t2)(30 + 3t) B) R(t) = (1500 + 400t + 30t2) + (30 + 3t) C) R(t) = (1500 + 400t + 30t2) - (30 + 3t) D) R(t) = (1500 + 400t + 30t2) (30 + 3t)
